In this lesson, we're going to talk about maximization offer number three, the mid-tier add-on. You can see that this offer falls right after the soft add-on. Whether a customer takes the soft add-on or not, they're going to be brought to the mid-tier add-on offer. Let's talk about the elements of the mid-tier add-on offer. It's like before, it's got to be congruent, it's got to add value, and it can no way imply that the customer captivation offer is incomplete. The mid-tier is the typical upsell. This is what you typically see from the average marketer online, only in most sequences you see this far too early. This offer is priced between $297 and $497 with a three-pay option. Offerers are given the option of paying three payments or paying one payment. Let's talk about this three-payment option for a second. Let's say the offer is for $297. Let's say it's for $297, that's what you are charging as a single payment. When you do the three-pay option, what you want to do is you want to make the total slightly higher than the single pay. If the single pay is $297, you would have the three-pay option at maybe something like $109. That the three-pay option, it's less obviously per payment, but the total is more than the $297. In a lot of cases, even if the three-pay option is more total wise than the single pay, you will get a substantial number of people that will take the three-pay option over the single pay option. Again, this is priced at $297 to $497 with a three-pay option. This is presented as either a VSL or a long-form sales letter. This does have a delayed add to cart button. Obviously, if it's a long-form sales letter, it's not going to have a delayed add to cart button. If it's presented as a VSL, which is what I recommend you do, it will be presented with a delayed add to cart button where the add to cart button will pop up when the offer is presented. I want you to recognize a couple of things. I want you to recognize why this offer converts as well as it does at this particular point in the sequence. An individual that has taken the customer captivation offer, the reverse add-on and the soft add-on has proven themselves to be a buyer. It makes adding this, especially with a three-pay option, to their order a lot more likely. The reason why we present it with a delayed add to cart button is because at the higher price point, it requires a bit more marketing to make the sale happen. It requires a longer presentation because odds are there are going to be more features, advantages, and benefits to present. There are going to be more objections, if you will, to overcome. There's going to be more marketing that needs to happen at this price point compared to a $49 price point or a $67 price point. Because of that, we want to communicate what we want to communicate without displaying the price right away. That is maximization offer number three.
